raw_consumer_statement:
I rented a vehicle through the XXXX XXXX, which showed a total rental cost of {$2000.00}, and I paid that amount in full using my Citi credit card. After the rental, the total suddenly ballooned to over {$8500.00}. The base rental rate was changed, a $ XXXX loss damage waiver ( insurance ) was added without my authorization, and the taxes and fees were recalculated and inflated based on those unauthorized changes. None of these additions were ever disclosed or agreed to in the app. 
I disputed only the overcharges not the original {$2000.00} that I paid and acknowledged. Despite this, Citi treated the dispute as if I had contested the entire amount. Citi initially credited my account while investigating, but later reversed that credit, claiming XXXX provided valid documentation. I was never shown that documentation, nor was I given proof that the charges matched what was displayed in the XXXX app at booking. 
I submitted phone recordings, emails, and documentation to both Citi and XXXX showing that my original liability was {$980.00} and that no insurance was ever selected. In one call, a XXXX representative confirmed their company did not receive the funds from the chargeback, directly contradicting Citis claim that the merchant retained the money. 
Despite all this, Citi closed the dispute without addressing the changed rental rate, unauthorized insurance, or inflated taxes and fees. Im asking the CFPB to ensure Citi reopens and properly investigates this case, as I acted in good faith, paid the correct amount, and was held responsible for over {$6000.00} in unauthorized and unexplained charges.
